title: Medows demonstrates an AI-assisted clinical workspace for ward rounds
friendlyTitle: Medows shows an AI workspace for hospital ward teams
summary: Medows has published an interactive demonstration of a clinical workspace that organises ward patients, vital signs, status changes and handover tasks in one interface. The demo positions AI as support for reviewing and coordinating a shift rather than as an autonomous diagnosis system.
friendlySummary: The Medows demo brings patient lists, vital signs, alerts and handover work into one screen. It is designed to help clinicians organise a shift.
whyItMatters: Clinical teams lose time moving between fragmented records and handovers, so a coherent workspace could improve attention and continuity. Real adoption still depends on validated accuracy, privacy, integration and clear clinician responsibility for every decision.
friendlyWhyItMatters: A clearer workspace may save time and reduce missed information. Hospitals still need to test accuracy, protect patient data and keep clinicians in charge.
